Good morning, I芒聙聶m Dan Gartland. I hope all the triathletes brought some Pepto Bismol with them to the Olympic Village.

In today芒聙聶s SI:AM:

冒聼楼聡Gold again for U.S. women芒聙聶s gymnastics冒聼陇聰A 芒聙聹slow pool芒聙聺 in Paris?冒聼聰聞MLB trade deadline wrap-up

One of the biggest questions entering these Olympic Games was whether organizers芒聙聶 bold plan to hold the swimming portion of the triathlon in the Seine would backfire. On Wednesday, after months of doubts over the quality of the water in the river, both the men芒聙聶s and women芒聙聶s races went off as planned. Mostly.

For background, the decision to use the Seine for the swimming leg of the triathlon芒聙聰and the open-water marathon swim next week芒聙聰was one of the most controversial aspects of these Games. If it worked out, it would lead to the spectacular sight of a pack of more than 50 athletes swimming right through the heart of Paris. But there was a big problem:the quality of the water.

The Seine has long been a highly polluted waterway, and getting it Olympics-ready was a difficult and very expensive undertaking. Paris spent $1.5 billion on a variety of projects designed to improve the water quality. That included upgrades to water treatment plants, renovating the city芒聙聶s 14th-century sewer system and building a catch basin designed to prevent untreated waste from entering the river.

And the project mostly worked. Water quality in the Seine did improve, but it was still highly variable. After heavy rains,the amount of E. coli and Enterococci bacteria in the waterwould increase. In the months before the Games, the bacteria levels in the river were still too high for safe swimming, but organizers hoped that drier, sunnier summer weather would work in their favor. It didn芒聙聶t turn out that way.

The heavy rain that viewers saw during the opening ceremony on Friday continued into Saturday and caused bacteria levels to spike. The planned triathlon practice sessions scheduled for Sunday and Monday were canceled due to the water quality and the men芒聙聶s race, originally scheduled for Tuesday, had to be postponed.

That led to a triathlon doubleheader on Wednesday, with the women going off at 8 a.m. local time as planned and the men following at 10:45 a.m. local time. France芒聙聶s Cassandre Beaugrand took gold in the women芒聙聶s race, followed by Switzerland芒聙聶s Julie Derron in second and Great Britain芒聙聶s Beth Potter in third. Great Britain芒聙聶s Alex Yee won the men芒聙聶s race, with New Zealand芒聙聶s Hayden Wilde and France芒聙聶s L脙漏o Bergere taking silver and bronze, respectively.

It was a relief for all the athletes, who had been upset about the possibility that the swimming portion of the race could be scrapped entirely and the event changed to a duathlon. But we芒聙聶ll wait to see if organizers were right to hold the race as planned.

Paris 2024 and World Triathlon said in a joint statement before the first race that they had been given the go-ahead to proceed after receiving the results of a water quality test at 3:20 a.m. local time. But Paris organizing committee official Aur脙漏lie Merle revealed to reporters on Tuesday that the test samples used to determine the water quality are collected nearly 24 hours before the decision is made about whether it is safe to proceed with the swim. Thunderstorms rolled through Paris overnight Tuesday, after the water sample had been collected, so the water the athletes swam in Wednesday morning could have been more polluted than the most recent test results showed.

Beaugrand, the French winner of the women芒聙聶s race, dismissed concerns about the quality of the water.

芒聙聹I have no doubts about the quality of the Seine,芒聙聺 she said. 芒聙聹We芒聙聶ve swum in worse water.芒聙聺

But that attitude was not shared by all of her colleagues. American Taylor Spivey, who finished 10th in the women芒聙聶s race,told NBC Newsthat she swallowed 芒聙聹a ton of water.芒聙聺

芒聙聹I芒聙聶ve taken a lot of probiotics over the past month,芒聙聺 she added. 芒聙聹So we芒聙聶ll see how it goes.芒聙聺

South Africa芒聙聶s Jamie Riddle, the 25th-place finisher in the men芒聙聶s race, left less to the imagination.

芒聙聹I芒聙聶m definitely going to be visiting the bathroom later,芒聙聺 he told reporters,according to Reuters. 芒聙聹I swallowed gallons and gallons of water, so that芒聙聶s gonna be a fun post-race party.芒聙聺
